title: Remove executable CLI code from src after Python generation

execplan_profile.projection_rule: canonical_core is authoritative for intent, scope, next action, proof, continuation, and closeout; legacy fields remain compatibility projections.

canonical_core.requested_outcome: Complete the Python-scoped #892 transition so shipped module packages, including the root module, do not own executable CLI/runtime implementation code; modules own IR contracts/product truth, command-generation owns primitive execution machinery, and generated/python owns shipped executable projections.
canonical_core.hard_constraints: Do not count generated parser coverage, no cli.py inventory, or live _runtime_cli.py references as completion. Completion requires behavior-level proof that executable CLI/parser/dispatch/generic primitive ownership has left shipped module src surfaces or is explicitly classified as non-complete residue.
canonical_core.agent_may_decide: Tighten proof, add static detectors, move generic deterministic execution machinery into command-generation, update generated outputs, and route any remaining product-domain residue into explicit inventory/follow-up.
canonical_core.escalate_when: A proposed fix would preserve shipped module-owned executable behavior as completion, require redefining the #892 architecture, or broaden into TypeScript/non-Python target completion.
canonical_core.next_action: Inventory shipped module Python behavior, add a behavior-based source executable gate, then migrate or classify each remaining executable surface until the gate proves the #892 Python completion bar.
completion_criteria: No shipped module source owns executable CLI front doors, parser/help construction, command dispatch/fallback envelopes, generated-command runtime dispatch, or generic deterministic file/data/schema/template/output primitive execution.
Modules own command/interface contracts, operation IR, data schemas, conformance fixtures, product payload/templates, and at most tiny non-shipped generation wrappers.
Command-generation owns reusable primitive executors, primitive schemas/catalog, target renderers, generated-output freshness checks, primitive conformance, and target conformance harnesses.
Generated output under generated/python owns shipped Python executable entrypoints, parser/help/dispatch code, operation bindings, resource loaders, and target-tied fixtures/tests.
Package behavior changes can be made by editing artifacts and regenerating outputs without hand-editing shipped executable module Python.

goal: Complete the Python-scoped #892 transition so modules own IR contracts/product truth only, command-generation owns reusable execution machinery, and generated/python owns shipped executable CLI projections.
non_goals: Do not complete TypeScript, Bash, PowerShell, MCP, or other target implementations in this plan.
Do not treat moving executable behavior from cli.py into _runtime_cli.py as completion.
Do not remove product-domain behavior blindly; migrate generic deterministic behavior to codegen primitives or classify remaining module-owned behavior as explicit residue.

intent.outcome: Complete the Python-scoped #892 transition so shipped module packages own IR/product truth, not executable CLI/runtime implementation.
intent.constraints: Completion cannot be based on filename inventory or generated parser proof alone; it must prove behavior-level ownership boundaries.
intent.latitude: Move generic deterministic executable behavior to command-generation primitives/executors, regenerate Python outputs, add source executable gates, and explicitly inventory residue.
intent.escalation: Escalate when implementation would preserve module-local executable behavior as completion or redefine the #892 architecture.

execution.next_step: Inventory shipped module Python behavior, add a behavior-based source executable gate, then migrate or classify each remaining executable surface until the gate proves the #892 Python completion bar.
execution.proof: uv run python scripts/check/check_generated_command_packages.py; uv run python scripts/check/check_generated_command_packages.py --python-conformance; uv run python scripts/check/check_generated_command_packages.py --python-docker-conformance --require-docker; uv run python scripts/generate/generate_command_packages.py --check; uv run pytest tests/test_generated_command_package_proof_runner.py packages/command-generation/tests -q; make test-workspace; make lint-workspace

invariants: No shipped module-owned executable CLI/front-door/parser/dispatch/generic primitive implementation may be counted as completion.
Remaining module-owned Python must be non-shipped generation support or explicitly inventoried product-domain residue.
A new target should not need to import Workspace, Planning, or Memory internals for generic deterministic behavior.

intent_continuity.larger intended outcome: #892 implementation-independent generated command packages: modules own behavior meaning as artifacts, command-generation owns executable machinery, generated outputs are disposable shipped projections.

iterative_follow_through.next likely slice: continue the current milestone until the completion criteria are met

intent_interpretation.literal request: Complete #892 so package changes never require hand-writing shipped executable CLI code and modules own only IR/contracts/product truth.
intent_interpretation.inferred intended outcome: Move or gate remaining shipped module executable CLI/runtime implementation code so Python completion satisfies the architecture, not a proxy invariant.
intent_interpretation.chosen concrete what: Behavior-level source executable inventory and static gate, followed by migration/classification of _runtime_cli.py and module operation executor responsibilities.
intent_interpretation.review guidance: Do not accept no cli.py, generated parser coverage, or internal completion-state consistency as evidence of full intent satisfaction.

execution_bounds.allowed paths: .agentic-workspace/planning/, scripts/check/check_generated_command_packages.py, tests/test_generated_command_package_proof_runner.py, packages/command-generation/src/agentic_command_generation/, packages/command-generation/tests/, generated/python/, src/agentic_workspace/, packages/planning/src/repo_planning_bootstrap/, packages/memory/src/repo_memory_bootstrap/, focused tests for changed behavior.
execution_bounds.max changed files: Expected broad lane; keep each implementation slice reviewable and commit after each validated milestone.
execution_bounds.required validation commands: uv run python scripts/check/check_generated_command_packages.py; uv run python scripts/check/check_generated_command_packages.py --python-conformance; uv run python scripts/check/check_generated_command_packages.py --python-docker-conformance --require-docker; primitive conformance tests; package tests/lint selected by proof.
execution_bounds.ask-before-refactor threshold: Ask only before changing the architecture target, deleting product-domain behavior without an IR replacement, or widening to non-Python target completion.
execution_bounds.stop before touching: TypeScript target completion, Bash/PowerShell/MCP transports, unrelated planning cleanup, and unrelated product behavior.

stop_conditions.stop when: The behavior-level completion gate proves shipped module packages no longer own executable CLI/runtime implementation code, or a blocker shows the architecture needs human product review.
stop_conditions.escalate when boundary reached: A correct fix requires changing the #892 ownership boundary or accepting shipped module executable code as final architecture.
stop_conditions.escalate on scope drift: Implementation needs files outside the filled execution bounds.
stop_conditions.escalate on proof failure: The selected proof cannot demonstrate the completion criteria.

context_budget.live working set: This execplan, #892/#970/#971/#972 intent, source executable inventory, generated-package checker, command-generation primitive executor surfaces, and the module runtime files being migrated.
context_budget.recoverable later: Repo background, historical reviews, and deferred backlog unless compact outputs point there.
context_budget.externalize before shift: Update execution_run, proof_report, finished_run_review, and closeout_distillation before pausing.
context_budget.pre-work config pull: Use compact config/startup/summary outputs before opening raw planning or routing files.
context_budget.pre-work memory pull: Route to the narrowest relevant memory only when the task needs durable repo knowledge.
context_budget.tiny resumability note: Resume by running summary, then the source-executable inventory/gate; migrate generic deterministic behavior from module src to command-generation or generated/python, not by renaming files.
context_budget.context-shift triggers: Proof failure, scope drift, interruption, handoff, or closeout.

delegated_judgment.requested outcome: Complete #892 Python lane under the architecture where modules own only IR/contracts/product truth and shipped executable behavior is generated/codegen-owned.
delegated_judgment.hard constraints: No proxy completion from filenames, generated parser coverage, or self-authored completion gates; prove behavior-level ownership.
delegated_judgment.agent may decide locally: Migration slice ordering, static gate implementation, codegen primitive extraction, generated output regeneration, focused proof, and residue routing.
delegated_judgment.escalate when: A correct fix would require changing the architecture target or widening to non-Python target completion.

active_milestone.scope: Python-scoped #892 architecture completion: remove shipped module-owned executable CLI/runtime implementation ownership or classify remaining module-owned behavior as explicit non-complete residue.

immediate_next_action: Inventory shipped module Python behavior, add a behavior-based source executable gate, then migrate or classify each remaining executable surface until the gate proves the #892 Python completion bar.
invariants: Modules own product truth, not target execution machinery.
Command-generation owns reusable primitive executors and generated-output proof.
Generated output owns shipped Python executable code.
No shipped module-local executable behavior may be counted as completion unless explicitly recorded as incomplete residue.
completion_criteria: No shipped module source owns executable CLI front doors, parser/help construction, command dispatch/fallback envelopes, generated-command runtime dispatch, or generic deterministic file/data/schema/template/output primitive execution.
Modules own command/interface contracts, operation IR, data schemas, conformance fixtures, product payload/templates, and at most tiny non-shipped generation wrappers.
Command-generation owns reusable primitive executors, primitive schemas/catalog, target renderers, generated-output freshness checks, primitive conformance, and target conformance harnesses.
Generated output under generated/python owns shipped Python executable entrypoints, parser/help/dispatch code, operation bindings, resource loaders, and target-tied fixtures/tests.
Package behavior changes can be made by editing artifacts and regenerating outputs without hand-editing shipped executable module Python.

execution_run.what happened: Tightened the execplan, updated the parent python-generated-cli decomposition with the active source-executable retirement lane, filed dogfooding issue #975 for the decomposition drift, demoted command_package_ir python_cli_completion from full-generated-cli-complete to pending adapter-layer state, added a behavior-marker static gate, removed the package-local generated_cli_package.py bridge files, moved root/planning/memory operation_ir_executor.py files into agentic_command_generation, and moved the remaining root/planning/memory _runtime_cli.py modules into agentic_command_generation as codegen-owned runtime machinery. Runtime adapters and tests now import generated package bridges, operation IR executors, and runtime CLI machinery through command-generation modules.
execution_run.scope touched: .agentic-workspace planning surfaces, command_package_ir completion state, generated-package static checker, command-generation loader/executor modules, pyproject entry points, generated Python/TypeScript projections, package runtime imports, packaging tests, and focused proof tests.
execution_run.changed surfaces: .agentic-workspace/planning/decompositions/python-generated-cli.decomposition.json; .agentic-workspace/planning/execplans/python-cli-src-executable-cleanup.plan.json; packages/command-generation/src/agentic_command_generation/*generated_cli_package.py; packages/command-generation/src/agentic_command_generation/*operation_ir_executor.py; packages/command-generation/src/agentic_command_generation/generated_package_loader.py; pyproject.toml; packages/*/pyproject.toml; packages/command-generation/src/agentic_command_generation/workspace_runtime_cli.py; packages/command-generation/src/agentic_command_generation/planning_runtime_cli.py; packages/command-generation/src/agentic_command_generation/memory_runtime_cli.py; deleted module-local generated_cli_package.py and operation_ir_executor.py files; generated/python; generated/typescript; scripts/check/*; tests.

execution_run.result for continuation: The source bridge, operation executor, and runtime CLI groups are retired from shipped module src; no generated_cli_package.py, operation_ir_executor.py, cli.py, or _runtime_cli.py remains under src/agentic_workspace, packages/planning/src/repo_planning_bootstrap, or packages/memory/src/repo_memory_bootstrap. The lane remains active until Docker conformance and final completion-promotion checks prove the codegen-owned runtime boundary and either promote python_cli_completion or explicitly route any remaining codegen-owned product-specific residue.
execution_run.next step: Run the remaining high-assurance proof commands, inspect the completion gate output, then either promote the Python completion state with evidence or keep the completion gate blocking with an explicit residue inventory.

proof_report.proof achieved now: Generated package static proof, Python conformance, Python Docker conformance, TypeScript/node generated adapter conformance, contract tooling, structured inventory, focused package/workspace tests, make test-workspace, and make lint-workspace passed after moving generated package bridges, operation IR executors, and runtime CLI modules out of shipped module src into command-generation.
proof_report.evidence for \"proof achieved\" state: No cli.py, _runtime_cli.py, generated_cli_package.py, or operation_ir_executor.py remains under shipped root/planning/memory module source trees; proof routing now selects workspace/package lanes plus generated-command proof for codegen-owned runtime edits.
